creator: roam@orbitel.bg
text:
The GNU ftp site changed its structure - now, the so-called "non-GNU software"
is not in pub/gnu/%SUBDIR%/, but in pub/gnu/non-gnu/%SUBDIR%/ (and do not
ask me what is "non-GNU software" at the GNU site..)

Here's a little attempt at a patch which duplicates every site listed in
MASTER_SITES_GNU, and tries the non-gnu/ path too.  I know this is a bit
cumbersome, but I can think of no other solution :(

[two minutes pass..]

Well, actually there *is* another solution - along with MASTER_SITES_GNU,
create a new sites entry - MASTER_SITES_QUASI_GNU or something ;), which
also lists all the GNU mirrors, but points at the non-gnu/ subdir.

The more I think about it, the more this seems to be the better way;
each port knows whether it's a GNU or a non-GNU port, and uses the proper
site list.  Alright, so I'm also attaching a second patch, which tries
to do this :)  Now all that needs to be done is for someone to go through
all the ports trying to use MASTER_SITES_GNU, and determine whether it's
an antelope or a plain old horse..

How-To-Repeat: 
Try to fetch e.g. the games/nethack port from the GNU site - it is now
in /pub/gnu/non-gnu/nethack/, which is a bit of a surprise to 'fetch'.
